description:
You are acting as a database administrator who wants a lightweight local shell configuration that makes query-tuning easier when using the PostgreSQL command-line client (psql).  
    Perform the following configuration steps from the regular, non-root account located at /home/user:

    1. Directory  
       • Create a new directory strictly at  
         /home/user/db_admin/conf  

    2. PostgreSQL start-up file  
       • Inside that directory create a file named .psqlrc whose **entire** textual content must be exactly the three lines shown below, in the same order, each terminated by a single Unix newline (LF) character and **nothing else**:  
         \timing on  
         \pset pager off  
         \set HISTSIZE 5000  

    3. Shell environment variable  
       • Ensure that the following line is present somewhere (once only) in the file /home/user/.bashrc:  
         export PSQL_EDITOR="nano"  

    4. Verification log  
       • Generate the file /home/user/db_admin/setup.log with the four lines shown below, in the same order, each terminated by a single Unix newline (LF) and with no additional blank lines or spaces:  
         PSQL_EDITOR=nano  
         TIMING=on  
         PAGER=off  
         HISTSIZE=5000  

    Output format that will be tested:  
    • The automated tests will read the contents of /home/user/db_admin/conf/.psqlrc and expect exactly the three lines shown above with no extra whitespace.  
    • The tests will grep /home/user/.bashrc for the literal string export PSQL_EDITOR="nano".  
    • The tests will read the contents of /home/user/db_admin/setup.log and expect exactly the four lines listed in item 4.  

    You do NOT need root privileges; everything lives inside /home/user.  
    Hint: all of the above can be completed with a very small set of shell commands (mkdir, echo, printf, etc.).  
    When you are done, no further console output is required—the presence and exact content of the files will be used for grading.
truth:
[Directory]  
    • /home/user/db_admin/conf   → must exist.

    [File] /home/user/db_admin/conf/.psqlrc  
    Exact byte sequence (including final newline):  
    \timing on\n  
    \pset pager off\n  
    \set HISTSIZE 5000\n  

    [File] /home/user/.bashrc  
    • Must contain at least one line exactly equal to: export PSQL_EDITOR="nano"

    [File] /home/user/db_admin/setup.log  
    Exact byte sequence (including final newline):  
    PSQL_EDITOR=nano\n  
    TIMING=on\n  
    PAGER=off\n  
    HISTSIZE=5000\n
